mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 9% indicates substantial labor slack, which depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE is above 9% over any 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, the zero lower bound signals prolonged accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and supporting equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in a majority of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over any 1-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in housing starts and industrial production reflects broad demand contraction, which reduces corporate profitability and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from HOUST and INDPRO fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC below 0 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which strengthens consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: Declining unemployment regime (UNRATE falls by at least 0.2% over 3 months)
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative more than half the time when UNRATE is trending downward over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales above $390,000 million indicate robust consumer spending, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) in periods when RSAFS exceeds 390,000.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of near-zero interest rates and high unemployment reflects a liquidity trap and demand deficiency, which depresses economic growth and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: Liquidity trap regime (low rates and high unemployment)
failure_condition: If the interaction term (FEDFUNDS * UNRATE) does not predict negative returns with a success ratio above 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production below 95 indicates weak manufacturing activity, which signals broader economic softness and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive when INDPRO is below 95 in more than 40% of observations over a 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 600,000 units suggest a housing market recovery, which stimulates construction employment and consumer wealth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: Housing market expansion (HOUST increasing from trough)
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is above 600,000 and sequentially rising.
